branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The Cathedral & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Red Sox pride
branding.idle_msg: No Red Sox game in progress

columnists.name: Francis 'Frankie' Sheehan
columnists.backstory: A Dorchester reporter who covered the 2004 Sox from the curb when they broke the curse and has never once called it 'the curse' in print.
columnists.voice_sample: Devers homered, Houck pitched into the seventh, and Boston beat the Yankees 5-3 at Fenway Park. Record now 9-8.

columnists.name: Maureen 'Mo' Kilbane
columnists.backstory: A Southie bakery owner who thinks every Red Sox prospect is the next Pedro and says so with her whole chest.
columnists.signature_phrase: Pahk the cah at Fenway Pahk.
columnists.voice_sample: Triston Casas is a goddamn monument waiting to happen. By July that lumber is going into the Mass Pike, I swear on my grandmother's cannoli.

columnists.name: Artie Gavrilov
columnists.backstory: A Revere retired pipefitter who never forgave the Mookie Betts trade and keeps the trade receipt on his kitchen fridge.
columnists.signature_phrase: John Henry sold the team, bub.
columnists.voice_sample: Mookie's in LA hitting walk-offs and we're hoping some kid from Pawtucket hits .260. This is what betrayal looks like in a jersey.
